make slides for the client meeting tomorrow

I need to present these findings to the VP of Enrollment
create a deck that explains our scoring system improvements to the client
turn this data analysis into a client-facing proposal
make a presentation for the admissions team about the new features we built
prepare a slide showing what we recommend and estimated hours for the client
put together a proposal for the client based on the BigQuery results
I need an HTML slide to email to the client summarizing our work
create a non-technical presentation of our model optimisation findings
build a stakeholder proposal with activation steps for each recommendation

review this pull request for the scoring model code
run the data analysis on enrollment rates in BigQuery

write a Slack summary of today's standup meeting

make a slide but it's for an internal team retrospective not a client
create a presentation about our tech stack for a conference talk
create a one-pager proposal for the client about our enrollment model
present ML findings to a technical CTO who wants the model details
propose changes to the client but I only have verbal context no files
translate our BQ analysis into something the admissions VP can understand
make a client proposal deck in a different language for non-English stakeholders

test_cases.prompt: I just finished analyzing enrollment data in BigQuery. Can you make a proposal slide for the admissions VP showing what we found and what we recommend?

assertions.description: Asks about audience and decision-makers before building
Asks what decision this proposal needs to drive
Does NOT immediately start generating HTML
Plans to produce three HTML versions plus PDF

test_cases.prompt: Here is the analysis JSON with enrollment rates. Just make the slide, skip the questions.

assertions.description: Pushes back once on skipping discovery — asks about audience
Does not include ML jargon on client slide
Splits recommendations into two confidence tiers
Includes the exploratory tier for uncertain items

test_cases.prompt: Create a proposal for expanding Salesforce data integration. The audience is the enrollment marketing director and the IT CRM admin. They need to approve budget for Q3.

assertions.description: Does not re-ask audience since it was provided
Asks remaining activation questions about stakeholder workflows
Includes estimated hours per proposed item
Asks about status quo cost since not provided

test_cases.prompt: Turn our model accuracy improvements into a client deck. AUC went from 0.72 to 0.89 and we added 5 new features.

assertions.description: Does NOT put AUC numbers on the client slide
Does not include raw accuracy numbers
Replaces 'features' with 'signals' or 'data points'
Includes business activation section for each recommendation

test_cases.prompt: We need to present the propensity model results to both the CIO and the admissions counselors in the same meeting.

assertions.description: Suggests producing multiple versions for different audiences
Includes operational activation for counselors
Produces HTML output files
Includes strategic framing appropriate for CIO

test_cases.prompt: Build the final proposal slide for our enrollment model improvements. Audience is VP Admissions, decision is budget approval for Q4, activation is counselor outreach lists.

assertions.description: Produces all three HTML layout versions
Generates a PDF version for sharing
Uses 16:9 aspect ratio at 1280x720
Delegates HTML generation to frontend-design skill

edge_cases.input: Make a slide but I don't have any analysis data yet — I just want to show the client what we COULD do
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Adapts to speculative/scoping mode. Frames all proposals as Areas We Could Explore, not Model Optimisation. Does not fabricate evidence or metrics. Still asks discovery questions about audience and decision.

assertions.description: Does not claim validated results without data
Frames everything as exploratory

edge_cases.input: The client wants this in PowerPoint format, not HTML
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Produces HTML slides at 1280x720 and explains they can be screenshotted into PowerPoint or exported via PDF. Does not attempt to generate .pptx files directly.

assertions.description: Still produces HTML as primary output
Does not promise native PowerPoint generation

edge_cases.input: Create a proposal but the audience is highly technical — the CTO wants to see actual model metrics and architecture details
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Adjusts language rules to allow more technical depth for a technical audience, while still maintaining the activation framework and two-tier confidence structure.

assertions.description: Still includes business activation framing even for technical audience
Still uses two-tier confidence structure

edge_cases.input: I want to add a competitive analysis section and a timeline roadmap to the proposal slide
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Accommodates custom sections within the existing layout structure. Restructures the grid to include additional content while maintaining the core narrative flow.

assertions.description: Discusses restructuring the layout for custom sections
Maintains core narrative structure

edge_cases.input: Make the slide but don't use the frontend-design skill — just give me simple plain HTML
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Respects the preference but notes that frontend-design produces higher visual quality. Produces functional HTML with inline styles following all content and structure rules.

assertions.description: Produces working HTML without frontend-design
Uses inline styles when frontend-design is skipped

edge_cases.input: Create a proposal slide from this data but the numbers seem inconsistent — CRM says 500 students but the model data shows 1200 observations
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Flags the discrepancy before proceeding. Applies cautious attribution rules. Uses rates/percentages rather than raw counts to avoid deduplication ambiguity.

assertions.description: Uses cautious language for conflicting data
Prefers rates over raw counts

edge_cases.input: Generate the proposal but I need it in under 5 minutes, no discovery questions
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Pushes back once on skipping discovery, then proceeds with reasonable defaults. Flags assumptions clearly. Produces all outputs but notes that the proposal quality depends on context.

assertions.description: Flags assumptions when skipping discovery
Uses reasonable defaults

edge_cases.input: Build a proposal slide but the analysis file is empty — it's a 0 byte JSON
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Detects empty input gracefully. Asks the user to provide analysis data or describe findings verbally. Does not generate a slide with fabricated content.

assertions.description: Does not generate HTML from empty data
Asks user to provide actual data
